context: Binary search implementation for a sorted list.

ground_truth_issues.message: Right boundary should be 'len(arr) - 1', not 'len(arr)'. This causes an off-by-one that can raise IndexError on the last element.
Setting 'left = mid' when arr[mid] < target causes an infinite loop when left + 1 == right, because mid == left and left never advances.
Setting 'right = mid' when arr[mid] > target is also incorrect; it should be 'right = mid - 1' to make progress.

context: API pagination: compute offset from page number and page size.

ground_truth_issues.message: Off-by-one bug: offset should be (page - 1) * page_size without adding 1, otherwise the first row of page 1 is skipped.
SQL LIMIT and OFFSET parameter order may be swapped relative to the query placeholders — verify against your DB driver API.
ground_truth_issues.suggestion: Pass arguments in the same order as placeholders (LIMIT then OFFSET).

context: Check whether two date ranges [a_start, a_end) and [b_start, b_end) overlap.
code_snippet:
from datetime import date

def ranges_overlap(a_start: date, a_end: date, b_start: date, b_end: date) -> bool:
    # Half-open intervals: [start, end)
    return a_start < b_end and b_start <= a_end

ground_truth_issues.message: Wrong boundary on b_start: for half-open intervals overlap requires b_start < a_end, not b_start <= a_end (adjacent ranges should not overlap).
ground_truth_issues.suggestion: return a_start < b_end and b_start < a_end

ground_truth_issues.message: Incorrect memoization: storing memo[n-1] before computing fib(n-2) breaks the recurrence; memo should store fib(n) only after both subproblems.

context: Merge two sorted arrays into one sorted array (ascending).
